title: Rap music used in evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Rap music used in evangelion             Quote Post by Rei`s Pimp AKA lil`Von » Tue Oct 13, 2009 10:31 am Now I have no problem with the type of music used in NGE (In fact my favorite scene is when Unit 01 is holding Kaworu) but what if hip-hop music was used instead. I think it would give eva a completly different feel. Any comments?

[]:             Quote Post by Ryjin » Tue Oct 13, 2009 10:57 am 1. See Evangelion: Vox 2. If hip-hop music was used instead would it give Eva a completely different feel? Ummmm, yah, while don't get my wrong I love hip hop with a passion (BUY IMMORTAL TECHNIQUES THIRD WORLD NOW!!!!) but Eva was going for for a retro 70 giant robot fell for it's music as well as theme for the fist couple of episodes. Could it have been used, well theoretically any music can work if done carefully but still I don't think I would really want rap music to be the main composition since hip hop tends to about inner city issues and themes which aren't really things dealt with in Eva. And besides It's main character is a quite kid from what could be seen as the Japanese equivalent of the burbs...Now Linkin Park on the other hand maybe.... (As I dunk and cover awaiting retaliation for trying to bring linkin park in to Eva) 3. And no offense but I think this topic should be in chit-chat.

[]: Re: Rap music used in evangelion             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Oct 13, 2009 11:00 am Rei`s Pimp AKA lil`Von wrote: but what if hip-hop music was used instead. I think it would give eva a completly different feel.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Oct 13, 2009 11:05 am This might be what it would feel like. (might be somewhere else on the board.)

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Tue Oct 13, 2009 11:08 am Ryjin wrote: 2. If hip-hop music was used instead would it give Eva a completely different feel? Ummmm, yah, while don't get my wrong I love hip hop with a passion (BUY IMMORTAL TECHNIQUES THIRD WORLD NOW!!!!) but Eva was going for for a retro 70 giant robot fell for it's music as well as theme for the fist couple of episodes. Could it have been used, well theoretically any music can work if done carefully but still I don't think I would really want rap music to be the main composition since hip hop tends to about inner city issues and themes which aren't really things dealt with in Eva. And besides It's main character is a quite kid from what could be seen as the Japanese equivalent of the burbs...Now Linkin Park on the other hand maybe.... (As I dunk and cover awaiting retaliation for trying to bring linkin park in to Eva)

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Tue Oct 13, 2009 11:49 am i'm not too big on rap/hip-hop, but personally I can see some of industrial style of music (Different subgenres of the genre can be used for certain styles of sequences) for the more intensely nightmarish/mindscrewish sequences (Excluding Komm, Susser Tod. That scene is perfect on all accounts), mixed in with the series' normal mix

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Oct 13, 2009 11:51 am Ryjin wrote: 2. If hip-hop music was used instead would it give Eva a completely different feel? Ummmm, yah, while don't get my wrong I love hip hop with a passion (BUY IMMORTAL TECHNIQUES THIRD WORLD NOW!!!!) but Eva was going for for a retro 70 giant robot fell for it's music as well as theme for the fist couple of episodes. Could it have been used, well theoretically any music can work if done carefully but still I don't think I would really want rap music to be the main composition since hip hop tends to about inner city issues and themes which aren't really things dealt with in Eva. And besides It's main character is a quite kid from what could be seen as the Japanese equivalent of the burbs...Now Linkin Park on the other hand maybe.... (As I dunk and cover awaiting retaliation for trying to bring linkin park in to Eva)

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Tue Oct 13, 2009 11:53 am I will agree that modern rap is just plain'.... old-school rap is much better. I visited a web page once that had a Wu-Tang song next to what I think was a Lil' Jon song. The Wu-Tang song was soothing while the other song was just ear rape but as I previously said, i'm more of a rock/metal/experimental music guy

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Tue Oct 13, 2009 12:46 pm i read the topic for this thread and i almost fell off my chair never would've imagined "rap" and "evangelion" could ever be in the same sentence my honest opinion? ummmm, NO. champloo could pull it off, but then again champloo was going for anachronistic, and rap music at least seems to fit one of the characters to some extent (mugen). i don't particularly think rap music fits all that way with the personality of any of the EVA characters... and if they had been trying to go with the "we'll use music with a feel that is completely opposite to what's actually happening on-screen just to screw with our audience's minds and freak them out royally" angle, i rather think they did a good job of that with the classical and children's music. i agree that it would certainly give the series a completely different feel... however i don't think it would be good different.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Oct 13, 2009 1:15 pm I'd rather see the whole thing scored by Jesus Jones .

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Oct 13, 2009 1:36 pm The only place I can see rap fitting into Evangelion in any way is a montage of a pimped out Gendo surrounded by Yui, Rei, Naoko, and Ritsuko, all of them dressed like prostitutes.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Oct 13, 2009 1:54 pm Insert random mental image of Toji strutting around singing (in bad Engrish) Ice-T's "99 problems" while Kensuke drags around a ridiculously huge '80's boombox.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Tue Oct 13, 2009 4:38 pm I like LP and some of the AMVs Ive seen with it done to EVA. (Stands his ground as the angry anti-LP-EVA mob approaches )

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Oct 13, 2009 9:02 pm NemZ wrote: Insert random mental image of Toji strutting around singing (in bad Engrish) Ice-T's "99 problems" while Kensuke drags around a ridiculously huge '80's boombox.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Tue Oct 13, 2009 9:34 pm NemZ wrote: I'd rather see the whole thing scored by Jesus Jones.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Oct 13, 2009 10:05 pm NemZ wrote: I'd rather see the whole thing scored by Jesus Jones .

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Tue Oct 13, 2009 10:09 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: NemZ wrote: I'd rather see the whole thing scored by Jesus Jones .
